title: Ponytail: The Lazy Senior Dev
description: How ponytail installs a lazy senior dev mindset into any AI coding agent

steps.title: The Lazy Senior Dev
steps.description: Ponytail installs a lazy senior dev into your AI agent. It enforces a 6-rung decision ladder—question need, standard-lib, platform features, existing deps, one-liner—before writing custom code. Result: 80 to 94% less code, 3 to 6x faster, 47 to 77% cheaper.

steps.title: The Six-Rung Ladder
steps.description: skills/ponytail/SKILL.md is the rule engine—the 6-rung ladder injected every response. Three modes: lite (suggest lazier alt), full (enforce default), ultra (YAGNI extremist). Companion skills cover /ponytail-review, /ponytail-audit, /ponytail-help.

steps.title: Session Lifecycle Hooks
steps.description: hooks/ is the runtime engine. hooks.json registers two lifecycle events: SessionStart injects the mode's rules; UserPromptSubmit tracks mode switches. ponytail-instructions.js reads SKILL.md and filters rules to the active intensity level.

steps.title: Multi-Platform Adapters
steps.description: Each platform gets a thin adapter wiring hooks/ and skills/ into its plugin system. .claude-plugin/ and .codex-plugin/ use plugin.json; gemini-extension.json hooks Gemini CLI; pi-extension/ is an ESM bridge; .opencode/ adds a .mjs plugin.

steps.title: Static Rules & Benchmarks
steps.description: Agents without plugin support (Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Copilot, Kiro) get static rule copies. scripts/check-rule-copies.js verifies they stay in sync. benchmarks/ holds a promptfoo eval suite that proved the 80 to 94% code-reduction claim.
